title: X11 Keyboards
text: X11 Keyboards X11 keyboards generate a set of codes called KEYSYM codes. X is responsible for the mapping from the physical keyboard codes to KEYSYM codes. Consequently, all X11 keyboards appear (to an application, if not the user) to be the same. The X consortium uses the same approach for application keyboard independence as Uniface. The X translation from physical to logical KEYSYM codes means that you do not need to use a variety of keyboard translation tables. Instead, you only need to use the default X11 keyboard translation table supplied with Uniface. If it is available, the xmodmap program can be used to edit or display the current settings for your X11 keyboard. Information about KEYSYM tables is beyond the scope of this manual. For more information, refer to your system documentation for X and xmodmap . The X Window System User’s Guide , Volume Three (ISBN 0-937175-14-5), written by Valeria Quercia and Tim O’Reilly, is also an excellent source of information. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
